description: Block marker text in body: when the payload content contains text that looks like same-pointer block markers (e.g., '[block /content bytes=1]' or '[endblock /content]'), the renderer must not be confused. Because blocks use byte-length delimited framing (not marker-based parsing), the marker-looking text inside the payload is harmless and transmitted verbatim.

input.content:
Some code:
[block /content bytes=1]
real data
[endblock /content]
done.

assertions.payload:
Some code:
[block /content bytes=1]
real data
[endblock /content]
done.
